commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=51ab30c6ada99699275fe259de498940f14cf2b3)
commands.review_summary:
The branch converts Doctor gateway service checks and adjacent repair buckets to finding-scoped structured repair output with dry-run changes, warnings, effects, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: a blocked active systemd service with remaining non-ExecStart drift exits before mutation, while the structured wrapper still emits an update effect; legacy cleanup can similarly be declined while the wrapper emits removal effects.
commands.review_followup: The protected draft needs maintainer sequencing and contributor live proof before any repair automation; the code findings are concrete but proof remains a human merge gate.

target.title: Doctor: structure gateway service repairs
target.body:
## Goal

Move gateway service Doctor repairs into the structured repair contract.

This wave converts gateway service detection, cleanup, service config repair, and platform service notes into finding-scoped Doctor repairs. Existing gateway repair policy stays intact, but the repair path now emits structured `changes`, `warnings`, `effects`, and dry-run data for later preview/reporting surfaces.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no runner note arrays.

## Legacy To Modern Shape

Gateway service repair has platform-specific behavior and historically carried presentation close to the service repair methods. This PR keeps the same service ownership boundaries while making the Doctor contract isomorphic:

- `detect()` reports service findings without mutation
- `repair()` receives the selected service findings and runs the existing repair operations
- dry-run describes service cleanup/config actions without changing service state
- repair effects make service operations visible to Doctor preview/reporting
- platform notes flow through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, not result payloads

## Converted Here

- gateway service detection
- service cleanup repairs
- service config repairs
- platform service notes
- gateway service tests for structured repair output and preserved behavior

## Review Surface

This is the gateway-policy PR to challenge:

- whether service cleanup, config repair, restart/status, and platform notes are split across the right methods
- whether gateway service effects should name service targets, config files, or platform actions differently
- whether dry-run should expose more concrete diffs for service config changes
- whether gateway-specific policy remains in gateway code instead of generic Doctor runner code

## Stack Position

Parent: #84326. This PR is the gateway service wave in the Doctor repair stack.

Follow-on PRs continue the same contract through session state, config audit/cron, and the top-level dry-run preview surface.

## Verification

- Restacked on #84326 as a single commit.
- Code search confirmed this branch does not reintroduce `HealthPresentationNote`, `repairNote`, `detectionNotes`, `repairNotes`, or structured note result surfaces.
- `git diff --check` passed during conflict resolution.
- Changed test coverage includes gateway service, plugin doctor, sandbox, health contribution, and repair-flow tests for this slice.
- Fresh PR CI should provide broad proof across the full stack.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway service Doctor repairs now use structured findings, finding-scoped repair, dry-run output, repair effects, and presentation-only notes while preserving existing service repair policy.

Real environment tested: Windows checkout on the refreshed stack.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check` during stack refresh; no focused Vitest command completed locally for this PR body refresh.

Evidence after fix: The branch updates gateway service doctor implementation and tests so service repairs report structured effects/changes/warnings and keep presentation notes out of result payloads.

Observed result after fix: Gateway repair behavior remains available through Doctor, while dry-run and later preview surfaces can reason over the same structured repair output.

What was not tested: Live gateway service repair scenarios and platform-wide Doctor CI were not rerun locally for this body refresh.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
